page: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the ESv4 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores running at 2.6 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is generally average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1409.58 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1225.0. However, multi-core performance falls into the weak category, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3699.56 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2720.0. Memory bandwidth tests show weak throughput, with read-only large block speeds at 24.02 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 18.05 GB/sec, though the PassMark memory score remains in the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of the E16-4s_v4 is its highly asymmetric resource allocation. While it offers an exceptionally large memory capacity of 128.0 GB, its processing power is limited to 4 vCPUs, resulting in weak multi-threaded execution and lower database operation benchmarks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server is designed for workloads that require high memory capacity but have low CPU demands, allowing users to avoid paying for unneeded CPU cores to obtain large RAM allocations.

Given these specifications, the server is suited for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel processing requirements. Appropriate workloads include in-memory caching, small-scale database operations that fit entirely in RAM, and memory-heavy text processing tasks. It is less suitable for CPU-bound tasks like software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, or LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the ESv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores clocked at 2.6 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show average single-core performance but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results, such as a stress-ng multi-core score of 3699.56 and large-block read speeds of 24.02 GB/sec. This asymmetric configuration offers high memory density relative to processing power, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for in-memory caching, text processing, and memory-constrained applications that do not require intensive parallel computation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v4 is an x86_64 server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads.
tagline: An asymmetric memory-optimized server combining a high 128 GB RAM capacity with a lightweight four-vCPU configuration for memory-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores
High memory density featuring 128.0 GB of RAM with 32.0 GB per core
No local storage, with support for Premium Storage configurations
Average single-core performance paired with weak multi-core processing capabilities
Best suited for in-memory caching, text processing, and memory-intensive, low-compute workloads
